title.en: BERT vs GPT: Pre-training & Fine-tuning Giants

description.en: Understand the key differences between BERT (bidirectional encoder) and GPT (autoregressive decoder), their pre-training objectives, and how to fine-tune them.
description.ta: BERT (bidirectional encoder) மற்றும் GPT (autoregressive decoder) இடையேயான முக்கிய வேறுபாடுகள், அவற்றின் முன்-பயிற்சி நோக்கங்கள், நுண்-சரிப்படுத்தும் முறைகளைப் புரிந்துகொள்ளுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Two Prodigies

Vaathiyaar introduces two brilliant students. "BERT reads the whole sentence at once — fill in the blanks. GPT reads left to right — predict the next word. Both learned from billions of words. Now you can teach them your specific task with just a little data."

### BERT: Bidirectional Encoder

```python
import torch
from torch import nn

# Simulating BERT-like masked language model
class MiniMLM(nn.Module):
    """Masked Language Model (BERT-style pre-training)"""
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, d_model=256, num_heads=8, num_layers=6):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, d_model)
        encoder_layer = nn.TransformerEncoderLayer(
            d_model=d_model, nhead=num_heads, dim_feedforward=1024,
            batch_first=True, dropout=0.1
        )
        self.encoder = nn.TransformerEncoder(encoder_layer, num_layers=num_layers)
        self.mlm_head = nn.Linear(d_model, vocab_size)
    
    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.embedding(x)
        x = self.encoder(x)
        return self.mlm_head(x)

# Pre-training: mask 15% of tokens, predict them
def mask_tokens(input_ids, vocab_size, mask_token_id, mask_prob=0.15):
    labels = input_ids.clone()
    mask = torch.rand(input_ids.shape) < mask_prob
    input_ids[mask] = mask_token_id  # replace with [MASK]
    labels[~mask] = -100             # only compute loss on masked tokens
    return input_ids, labels
```

### GPT: Autoregressive Decoder

```python
class MiniGPT(nn.Module):
    """Causal Language Model (GPT-style)"""
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, d_model=256, num_heads=8, num_layers=6, max_len=512):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, d_model)
        self.pos_embedding = nn.Embedding(max_len, d_model)
        decoder_layer = nn.TransformerDecoderLayer(
            d_model=d_model, nhead=num_heads, dim_feedforward=1024,
            batch_first=True, dropout=0.1
        )
        self.decoder = nn.TransformerDecoder(decoder_layer, num_layers=num_layers)
        self.lm_head = nn.Linear(d_model, vocab_size)
    
    def forward(self, x):
        seq_len = x.size(1)
        positions = torch.arange(seq_len, device=x.device).unsqueeze(0)
        x = self.embedding(x) + self.pos_embedding(positions)
        # Causal mask: can only attend to past
        mask = nn.Transformer.generate_square_subsequent_mask(seq_len).to(x.device)
        memory = torch.zeros_like(x)  # no encoder for decoder-only
        x = self.decoder(x, memory, tgt_mask=mask)
        return self.lm_head(x)
```

### Fine-tuning for Classification

```python
class BERTClassifier(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, pretrained_bert, num_classes):
        super().__init__()
        self.bert = pretrained_bert
        self.classifier = nn.Linear(256, num_classes)  # d_model=256
    
    def forward(self, x):
        encoded = self.bert.encoder(self.bert.embedding(x))
        cls_token = encoded[:, 0, :]  # [CLS] token representation
        return self.classifier(cls_token)
```

> **BERT:** Great for understanding (classification, NER, Q&A). **GPT:** Great for generation (text, code, chat).
story_variants.ta:
## வாத்தியாரின் இரண்டு மேதைகள்

வாத்தியார் இரண்டு அறிவாளி மாணவர்களை அறிமுகப்படுத்துகிறார். "BERT முழு வாக்கியத்தையும் ஒரே நேரத்தில் படிக்கிறது — வெற்றிடங்களை நிரப்புங்கள். GPT இடமிருந்து வலமாக படிக்கிறது — அடுத்த வார்த்தையை கணிக்கவும். இரண்டும் கோடிக்கணக்கான வார்த்தைகளிலிருந்து கற்றன."

steps.line: # BERT: mask 15% of tokens, predict them

explanation.en: BERT pre-training: randomly mask words, model predicts them using bidirectional context
Replace 15% of tokens with [MASK]. Model must use surrounding context to predict original word
Set non-masked positions to -100 (ignored by CrossEntropyLoss). Only train on masked tokens
BERT uses encoder (bidirectional attention — each token sees all other tokens)
GPT pre-training: predict the next token given all previous tokens. Autoregressive.
Causal mask: token at position i can only attend to positions 0..i (not future)
GPT uses decoder with causal masking — each token only sees past tokens
BERT fine-tuning: use [CLS] token (position 0) as the sentence representation
Add a small classification head on top of the pre-trained model
Modern LLMs scale from millions to hundreds of billions of parameters

left.label: BERT
left.content:
Bidirectional (sees all tokens)
Pre-training: Masked LM
Best for: classification, NER, QA
Encoder-only architecture
BERT-base: 110M params

right.label: GPT
right.content:
Autoregressive (left-to-right)
Pre-training: Next token prediction
Best for: text generation, chat
Decoder-only architecture
GPT-3: 175B params

layers.name: BERT Output
nodes: The cat sat on the mat

output: >>> output: 'The cat sat on the mat'
>>> output: 'on the mat because it was tired'

animation_sequence.code:
# Conceptual comparison of BERT vs GPT
# BERT: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers
# GPT: Generative Pre-trained Transformer

# BERT: Masked Language Modeling
sentence = 'The cat sat on the [MASK]'
# BERT sees ALL tokens, predicts masked ones
# Attention: each token attends to ALL others

# GPT: Causal Language Modeling
prompt = 'The cat sat on the'
# GPT only sees LEFT context, predicts next token
# Attention: each token attends to PREVIOUS tokens only

# Key differences:
comparison = {
    'BERT': {'direction': 'bidirectional', 'task': 'MLM', 'use': 'classification, NER'},
    'GPT':  {'direction': 'left-to-right', 'task': 'CLM', 'use': 'text generation'}
}
for model, info in comparison.items():
    print(f"{model}: {info['direction']}, {info['task']}, best for {info['use']}")

executionSteps.explanation: BERT masks random tokens and predicts them
BERT sees full context — left AND right of [MASK]
GPT predicts next token given left context
GPT uses causal mask — can only look at previous tokens

executionSteps.output:
BERT: bidirectional, MLM, best for classification, NER
GPT: left-to-right, CLM, best for text generation
executionSteps.explanation: BERT excels at understanding, GPT excels at generation

instruction.en: Implement a simplified BERT masked language model. Create a mini vocabulary, mask random tokens, train the model to predict masked tokens, and show the predictions.
instruction.ta: எளிமைப்படுத்தப்பட்ட BERT masked language model-ஐ செயல்படுத்துங்கள். mini vocabulary உருவாக்கி, random tokens-ஐ mask செய்து, மாதிரியை பயிற்சி செய்யுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

# Simple vocabulary
words = ['the', 'cat', 'sat', 'on', 'mat', 'dog', 'ran', 'in', 'park', '[MASK]', '[PAD]']
vocab = {w: i for i, w in enumerate(words)}
MASK_ID = vocab['[MASK]']

# Training sentences (as token indices)
sentences = [
    [vocab[w] for w in ['the', 'cat', 'sat', 'on', 'the', 'mat']],
    [vocab[w] for w in ['the', 'dog', 'ran', 'in', 'the', 'park']],
]

# TODO: Build MiniMLM (TransformerEncoder + Linear head)
# TODO: Implement masking function
# TODO: Train to predict masked tokens
# TODO: Show predictions: '[MASK] cat sat on the mat' -> 'the'
practice_challenges.expected_output:
Masked: '[MASK] cat sat on the mat'
Predicted: 'the' (correct!)

en: Mask 1-2 tokens per sentence during training
After training, feed masked input and argmax the output at masked positions
ta: பயிற்சியின் போது ஒவ்வொரு வாக்கியத்திலும் 1-2 tokens mask செய்யுங்கள்

q.en: What is the main difference between BERT and GPT pre-training?
q.ta: BERT மற்றும் GPT முன்-பயிற்சியின் முக்கிய வேறுபாடு என்ன?

en: BERT uses masked LM (bidirectional); GPT uses next-token prediction (left-to-right)
ta: BERT masked LM (bidirectional) பயன்படுத்துகிறது; GPT next-token prediction (left-to-right) பயன்படுத்துகிறது

q.en: For a text classification task, which model architecture is more natural?
q.ta: உரை வகைப்பாட்டுப் பணிக்கு, எந்த மாதிரி கட்டமைப்பு மிகவும் இயல்பானது?

q.en: What does fine-tuning a pre-trained model mean?
q.ta: முன்-பயிற்சி பெற்ற மாதிரியை fine-tune செய்வது என்ன பொருள்?

en: Training from scratch on new data
Continuing training on a specific task with a small dataset
ta: ஒரு குறிப்பிட்ட பணியில் சிறிய தரவுத்தொகுப்புடன் பயிற்சியைத் தொடர்தல்
